meta.title: Smooth Scroll Implementation Patterns for SOTY Event Horizon
meta.author: Cleanlystudio
meta.description: Comprehensive scroll pattern database covering Lenis, GSAP ScrollTrigger, and narrative scroll design

lenis.description: Lenis is a lightweight, performant smooth scroll library that normalizes scroll across browsers and devices
lenis.core_concept: Intercepts native scroll events and applies smoothing (lerp) to create buttery smooth scroll behavior

duration.description: Duration of the smooth scroll animation in seconds

easing.description: Easing function for scroll interpolation. Takes progress (0-1), returns eased value.

lerp.description: Linear interpolation factor. Lower = smoother/slower, higher = more responsive. Alternative to duration/easing.
lerp.note: Use either duration+easing OR lerp, not both

smoothWheel.description: Enable smooth scrolling for mouse wheel events

syncTouch.description: Apply smoothing to touch events. Usually false — touch should feel 1:1.
syncTouch.event_horizon_recommendation: false — touch scroll should be direct. Smoothing on touch feels laggy.

touchMultiplier.description: Multiplier for touch scroll speed. Higher = faster scrolling per swipe.

gsap_integration.description: Lenis must be connected to GSAP ScrollTrigger for scroll-driven animations to work
gsap_integration.critical_line: gsap.ticker.lagSmoothing(0) — disables GSAP's lag smoothing which conflicts with Lenis

scroller_proxy.description: For advanced setups, use ScrollTrigger.scrollerProxy to connect Lenis as the scroll source
scroller_proxy.when_needed: When using Lenis's wrapper/content pattern instead of native scroll
implementation: return { top: 0, left: 0, width: window.innerWidth, height: window.innerHeight };

raf_synchronization.description: Lenis must be called in a requestAnimationFrame loop synchronized with the render loop

patterns.gsap_ticker: gsap.ticker.add((time) => lenis.raf(time * 1000)) — synchronized with GSAP's frame loop
patterns.three_js: Call lenis.raf(time) inside Three.js's animation loop before rendering

raf_synchronization.recommendation: Use gsap.ticker for single-loop architecture — everything (Lenis, GSAP, Three.js render) in one rAF

scroll_to.description: Programmatically scroll to a position, element, or percentage
examples: lenis.scrollTo(0.5, { duration: 2 }) // scroll to 50% over 2 seconds
lenis.scrollTo(0, { immediate: true }) // jump to top instantly

on_scroll.example: lenis.on('scroll', ({ scroll, limit, velocity, direction, progress }) => { /* update UI */ })

stop_start.stop: lenis.stop() — disable scrolling (useful during modals, loading screens)

api.destroy: lenis.destroy() — clean up all event listeners and remove Lenis

touch_events.description: Lenis handles touch events for mobile smooth scrolling

behavior.syncTouch_false: Touch is 1:1 — native feel. Momentum is added after finger lift.
behavior.syncTouch_true: Touch is smoothed like wheel — can feel sluggish. Not recommended.

mobile_considerations: Mobile users expect 1:1 touch response — any smoothing feels like input lag
Touch momentum (overscroll after finger lift) should feel natural
Viewport is smaller — same scroll distance covers more content
Ensure touchMultiplier is calibrated for your content length

keyboard_accessibility.description: Lenis respects keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Page Up/Down, Home/End, Space)
keyboard_accessibility.default_behavior: Arrow keys, Page Up/Down, Home, End, and Space all work with smooth scrolling
keyboard_accessibility.custom: Can configure keyboard events or add custom key bindings for chapter navigation
keyboard_accessibility.event_horizon_recommendation: Add arrow key handlers for chapter-by-chapter navigation as an alternative to scroll

tips: Lenis itself is very lightweight (~5KB gzipped)
The smooth scroll adds no perceptible CPU overhead
Potential jank comes from WHAT happens during scroll (heavy animations, DOM updates), not from Lenis itself
On low-end devices, consider disabling smooth scroll entirely: new Lenis({ lerp: 1 }) — instant, no smoothing

gsap_scroll_trigger.description: GSAP ScrollTrigger connects animations to scroll position — the industry standard for scroll-driven animation

core_concepts.trigger: DOM element that defines when the ScrollTrigger activates
core_concepts.start_end: Scroll positions (relative to trigger and viewport) that define the active range
core_concepts.scrub: Links animation progress directly to scroll position
core_concepts.pin: Fixes an element in place while scroll continues

pin_behavior.description: Pins an element to the viewport while the user scrolls through a defined scroll distance
pin_behavior.how_it_works: Three.js inserts spacer elements to maintain document flow. The pinned element is fixed in the viewport. Scroll distance between start and end drives the animation.
pin_behavior.pin_spacing: By default, ScrollTrigger adds equivalent space below the pinned section. Set pinSpacing: false to disable (use when you manually manage layout).
pin_behavior.event_horizon_use: Each chapter is pinned. Scroll through 3000-5000px per chapter while the WebGL scene animates.

scrub.description: Links animation progress directly to scroll position

values.true: Instant — animation progress matches scroll position exactly (can feel jumpy with fast wheel events)
values.1: 1 second of catch-up smoothing — animation lags 1 second behind scroll
values.2: 2 second smoothing — very smooth but laggy feel

scrub.recommended: scrub: 1 for most animations. scrub: 0.5 for time-critical animations. scrub: true only for instant-response needs.
scrub.event_horizon_recommendation: scrub: 1 — smooth enough for cinematic feel, responsive enough to not feel disconnected

snap_points.description: After scroll stops, automatically snap to defined positions (chapter boundaries)

equal_sections: delay: 0.1 // wait 100ms after scroll stops before snapping
function_based: // Custom logic to find nearest snap point
return points.reduce((a, b) => Math.abs(b - progress) < Math.abs(a - progress) ? b : a);

snap_points.delay: Snap delay prevents snapping during active scrolling. 0.1-0.3s is typical.
snap_points.event_horizon_recommendation: Snap to chapter boundaries. Duration 0.4-0.6s. Delay 0.15s. This creates a 'chapter book' feel.

markers.description: Visual debug markers showing trigger positions
markers.usage: markers: true in ScrollTrigger config — shows colored lines in the viewport
markers.production: ALWAYS remove markers: true before production. It's a debug-only feature.

batch_animations.description: ScrollTrigger.batch() creates ScrollTriggers for multiple elements efficiently
batch_animations.use_case: Animating a list of items as they scroll into view with stagger
implementation: onEnter: (batch) => gsap.to(batch, { opacity: 1, y: 0, stagger: 0.1, duration: 0.5 }),

toggle_actions.description: Define what happens at each trigger boundary: onEnter, onLeave, onEnterBack, onLeaveBack

common_patterns.play_once: toggleActions: 'play none none none' — plays on enter, never resets
common_patterns.play_reverse: toggleActions: 'play none none reverse' — plays on enter, reverses on scroll back
common_patterns.play_pause: toggleActions: 'play pause resume pause' — plays/pauses based on visibility
common_patterns.restart_always: toggleActions: 'restart none none reverse' — always restart on re-enter

best_practices: Limit to 50 ScrollTriggers maximum — beyond this, the per-frame calculation overhead causes jank
Use batch() for repetitive element animations instead of individual ScrollTriggers
Use will-change: transform on pinned and animated elements
Avoid layout reads (getBoundingClientRect, offsetHeight) inside onUpdate callbacks
Use ScrollTrigger.refresh() after dynamically adding/removing content
For long pages, use lazy initialization — create ScrollTriggers only for nearby sections

too_many_triggers.solution: Consolidate into fewer, larger ScrollTriggers. Use timeline sub-animations within a single trigger.

heavy_on_update.problem: Complex computations in onUpdate callback run every frame
heavy_on_update.solution: Minimize onUpdate logic. Pre-compute values. Use GSAP tweens instead of manual updates.

layout_thrashing.problem: Reading DOM dimensions (offsetHeight, getBoundingClientRect) in scroll callbacks causes forced reflow
layout_thrashing.solution: Cache dimensions on init and resize. Never read layout during scroll.

scroll_linked_loading.problem: Loading images/assets on scroll causes jank and layout shifts
scroll_linked_loading.solution: Preload all assets before the experience starts. Use loading screen.

pinned_memory.problem: Many pinned elements can consume memory (pinned elements + spacers + duplicates)
pinned_memory.solution: Limit pinned elements to what's visible. Kill/recreate pins for off-screen sections.

long_page_scrollytelling.description: The entire narrative exists on a single long page, revealed through scrolling

architecture.chapter_height: 300-500vh per chapter (pinned, so scroll distance not actual DOM height)
architecture.padding: 10-20vh of 'breathing room' at start and end

advantages: Continuous experience — no page loads, no jumps
Scroll provides natural pacing control (user sets their own speed)
Easy to track progress (single scroll position)
disadvantages: Very long DOM can cause memory issues
Users may feel overwhelmed by the length
Hard to link to specific points within the experience

sticky_elements_with_changing_content.description: Fixed visual container with content that changes as the user scrolls
sticky_elements_with_changing_content.pattern: WebGL canvas is position: fixed. HTML text overlays change based on scroll position.

implementation.canvas: position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; z-index: 0;
implementation.scroll_driver: A tall empty div provides the scrollable area. Its height = total scroll distance needed.

sticky_elements_with_changing_content.event_horizon_application: The WebGL black hole scene is always visible (fixed). Text and HUD elements overlay on top and change with scroll.

progress_based_animations.description: Map scroll progress (0 to 1) to any visual property

examples.description: Orange to red as temperature rises
Text fades in at chapter start

chapter_based_navigation.description: Divide the experience into discrete chapters with visual navigation indicators

dot_navigation.description: Vertical dots on the side of the screen, one per chapter
dot_navigation.behavior: Active dot highlights current chapter. Click dot to scroll to that chapter.
dot_navigation.styling: Small (8-12px), subtle when inactive, bright when active
dot_navigation.accessibility: aria-label on each dot, keyboard focusable, role='navigation'

progress_bar.description: Thin horizontal or vertical bar showing scroll progress
progress_bar.position: Top of viewport (horizontal) or right edge (vertical)
progress_bar.behavior: Fills based on scroll position. May show chapter marks.
progress_bar.styling: 2-3px height, accent color (cyan), on dark background

chapter_counter.behavior: Updates as chapters change. Optional chapter title.

reduced_motion.description: Respect prefers-reduced-motion media query for users with vestibular disorders
implementation: If reduced motion: disable smooth scroll (use instant scroll)
Disable continuous animations (particle orbits, background drift)
Keep essential animations but remove decorative ones
Provide a 'Skip animations' option in the UI
reduced_motion.gsap_integration: ScrollTrigger.config({ limitCallbacks: true }) and reduce scrub smoothing to 0 (instant)
reduced_motion.critical: Awwwards judges test accessibility. Reduced motion support is expected for SOTY nominees.

viewport_size.impact: Mobile viewport is ~40% of desktop height. Same content requires 2.5x more scrolling.
viewport_size.solution: Reduce content per chapter on mobile. Show less text, fewer data points.

scroll_velocity.impact: Mobile scroll velocity is typically 2-3x higher than desktop (flick gestures)
scroll_velocity.solution: Use Lenis touchMultiplier to calibrate. Content transitions should be speed-aware.

overscroll_behavior.impact: iOS and Android have different overscroll (bounce/glow) behaviors that can interfere
overscroll_behavior.solution: CSS: overscroll-behavior: none; on html/body to prevent browser overscroll effects

address_bar.impact: Mobile browsers show/hide the address bar during scroll, changing viewport height
address_bar.solution: Use CSS: height: 100dvh (dynamic viewport height) instead of 100vh. Or use window.visualViewport API.

performance.impact: Mobile GPUs are 5-10x weaker than desktop. Heavy scroll callbacks cause frame drops.
performance.solution: Reduce ScrollTrigger count on mobile. Simplify animations. Lower render resolution.

single_loop_architecture.description: Run everything in a single requestAnimationFrame loop for synchronization
pattern: // Single loop that runs everything in sync
// 4. Update audio parameters based on scroll
why_single_loop: Multiple rAF callbacks can execute in different order between frames
Synchronization prevents visual tearing (scroll position updates in one frame, visuals in the next)
Easier to debug and profile (one stack trace per frame)
GSAP ticker is the natural hub — Lenis and Three.js rendering can be added to it

common_scroll_jank_causes.detection: Long task markers during scroll in DevTools Performance panel
common_scroll_jank_causes.fix: Move computation to GSAP tweens. Cache values. Debounce if non-visual.

common_scroll_jank_causes.detection: Forced reflow markers (purple blocks) in DevTools
common_scroll_jank_causes.fix: Cache all dimensions on init and resize. Never read layout during scroll.

common_scroll_jank_causes.detection: Paint regions (green overlay in DevTools) appearing during scroll
common_scroll_jank_causes.fix: Animate only transform and opacity. Add will-change: transform.

common_scroll_jank_causes.detection: Multiple event handlers in Chrome DevTools Event Listeners panel
common_scroll_jank_causes.fix: Consolidate into one handler or use passive: true. Let Lenis handle scroll normalization.

common_scroll_jank_causes.cause: WebGL render time exceeds frame budget
common_scroll_jank_causes.fix: Reduce draw calls, simplify shaders, lower resolution. See webgl-best-practices.json.

sources.title: 100vh on Mobile: The Lie We Tell
sources.author: CSS-Tricks
